output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to send a demand letter in 60 seconds.
content_outline: Is Your Landlord Illegally Withholding Your Security Deposit?
What Landlords Can and Cannot Deduct (By State Law)
State Deadlines: How Long Does a Landlord Have to Return Your Deposit?
How to Write a Security Deposit Dispute Letter That Gets Results
Escalating to Small Claims Court When Your Landlord Refuses
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 30 days with an itemized deduction list
Normal wear and tear, age-related depreciation, and pre-existing damage cannot be legally deducted
A formal letter of demand is often the fastest, cheapest way to recover withheld deposits without hiring a lawyer
If a landlord misses the state deadline or withholds without proper notice, tenants may be entitled to double or triple damages
output.cta: Don't let your landlord keep what's yours. Generate your state-specific security deposit demand letter at Deposits-Back.com for just $19.99 — no sign-up, no subscription, and done in 60 seconds.
output.target_audience: Frustrated US renters who have moved out and are experiencing a landlord withholding, delaying, or wrongfully deducting from their security deposit

social_hooks.hook: POV: You deep-cleaned the apartment, fixed every nail hole, and your landlord still kept your $2,000 deposit for 'cleaning fees'...
It's been 45 days since you moved out. No deposit. No itemized list. Just radio silence. Here's what they don't want you to know.
My landlord said I wouldn't get my deposit back because the carpet was 'worn.' It was 8 years old. That's normal wear and tear, Karen.
social_hooks.keyword_target: my landlord won't give me my deposit back

social_hooks.hook: Can your landlord keep your entire deposit for breaking a lease? Can they charge you for faded paint? Let's settle this once and for all.
If your landlord doesn't return your deposit within 30 days, they might owe you DOUBLE. Here's the 60-second fix before you call a lawyer.
social_hooks.keyword_target: what to do when landlord doesn't return deposit

short_form_scripts.title: The 30-Day Rule
short_form_scripts.script: [Hook] Your landlord has 21 to 30 days to return your deposit. Period. [Problem] If they don't send an itemized list of deductions, they're breaking the law. [Solution] You don't need a lawyer. You need a demand letter. Deposits-Back.com writes a state-specific letter in 60 seconds for $19.99. No sign-up. No subscription. Just your money back. Link in bio.

short_form_scripts.title: Normal Wear and Tear vs. Damage
short_form_scripts.script: [Visual: Split screen] THIS is damage: a hole in the wall. THIS is normal wear and tear: scuffed baseboards after three years. [Voiceover] Landlords love to confuse the two. If they're charging you for faded paint, old carpet, or tiny nail holes, that's likely illegal. [CTA] Fight back with a formal dispute letter at Deposits-Back.com. $19.99. 60 seconds. Get your deposit back.
